questions.q: What is the Kapi Dhwaja?
questions.explanation: Kapi Dhwaja, the monkey banner, flew above Arjuna's chariot throughout the war.

questions.q: Which divine being is emblazoned on, and resides in, the banner?
questions.explanation: Mighty Hanuman himself sat upon Arjuna's standard through the Kurukshetra War.

questions.q: What does the word 'kapi' mean?
questions.explanation: Kapi is Sanskrit for monkey — hence the monkey-bannered chariot.

questions.q: Which epithet of Arjuna comes directly from this banner?
questions.explanation: Arjuna is called Kapidhwaja, 'he of the monkey banner'.

questions.q: Hanuman is celebrated as the hero of which earlier epic?
questions.explanation: Hanuman, Rama's greatest devotee from the Ramayana, lived on into the Mahabharata's age.

questions.q: Whose chariot did the Kapi Dhwaja crown at the Bhagavad Gita's telling?
questions.explanation: The Gita opens on Arjuna's great chariot, the monkey banner streaming above.

questions.q: Which Pandava shared a divine father with Hanuman?
questions.explanation: Both Bhima and Hanuman are sons of Vayu, the wind god, making them brothers.

questions.q: What sound did Hanuman add from the banner during battle?
options: Terrifying roars that shook enemy hearts
questions.explanation: The epic says fierce cries from the banner's beings unnerved the Kaurava ranks.

questions.q: Who drove the chariot that bore the Kapi Dhwaja?
questions.explanation: Krishna held the reins of Arjuna's chariot beneath the monkey banner.

questions.q: The chariot under the banner was drawn by horses of what color?
questions.explanation: Arjuna's four white steeds gave him the name Shwetavahana, 'he of the white horses'.

questions.q: In the epic's well-known forest episode, to whom did Hanuman reveal himself before the war?
options: Bhima, on the path to the saugandhika flowers
questions.explanation: Hanuman blocked Bhima's path as an old monkey whose tail Bhima could not lift, then revealed his true self.

questions.q: What did Hanuman promise during that forest meeting?
options: To fight beside the Pandavas in person
To roar from Arjuna's banner and strike terror into the enemy
To carry messages across the sea
questions.explanation: Hanuman pledged his presence on Arjuna's standard, his war cries multiplying the army's dread.

questions.q: Why couldn't Bhima lift the old monkey's tail from his path?
options: The monkey was Hanuman, whose strength no mortal could match
A curse had weakened Bhima that day
The tail was rooted to the mountain
Bhima feigned weakness out of respect
questions.explanation: The humbling of proud Bhima by his brother Hanuman is the famous lesson of the episode.

questions.q: At Agni's request, who handed over the divine chariot that bore the banner?
questions.explanation: Before the Khandava burning, Agni summoned Varuna, who gave Arjuna the Gandiva, inexhaustible quivers, and the celestial ape-bannered chariot once kept for Soma.

questions.q: In the traditional telling, what protective role did Hanuman's presence play for the chariot itself?
options: It made the chariot invisible at will
It let the chariot fly over broken ground
It shielded the chariot from celestial weapons all through the war
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that with Hanuman above and Krishna at the reins, the chariot absorbed astra after astra without breaking.

questions.q: What happened to Arjuna's chariot at the war's end when Krishna finally dismounted?
options: It burst into flames and burned to ash
questions.explanation: Once Hanuman departed and Krishna stepped down, the astra-riddled chariot was consumed by fire.

questions.q: In what order did Krishna insist they leave the chariot on that final day?
questions.explanation: Krishna had Arjuna alight first; only then did he leave, and the chariot blazed up behind them.

questions.q: What did Krishna explain about why the chariot survived until that moment?
options: The Gandiva's power had preserved it
His own presence and the banner's guardian had held its destruction at bay
questions.explanation: Krishna revealed the chariot had long been destroyed by divine weapons — only he and Hanuman had kept it whole.

questions.q: Which weapon's earlier strikes were specifically said to have doomed the chariot?
options: Divine astras of Drona and Karna, including Brahma weapons
questions.explanation: The fires of the great astras hurled by Drona and Karna had already consumed it in essence.

questions.q: What lesson is traditionally drawn from the chariot's burning?
options: That war destroys even its instruments
That without the divine, even the mightiest equipment is ash
That banners must be renewed each war
questions.explanation: The episode dramatizes total dependence on grace — remove the divine and nothing of power remains.

questions.q: Besides Kapidhwaja, which other epithet of Arjuna also comes from his monkey banner?
questions.explanation: Vanara-ketu and Kapi-ketana likewise mean 'monkey-bannered'; the standard itself was said to blaze like fire, visible from a league away.

questions.q: How did enemy warriors reportedly react to the banner's cries on the field?
options: They answered with their own war songs
Their mounts and hearts quailed before the unearthly roars
They could not hear them over the conches
questions.explanation: The shrieks of the banner's fierce beings froze men and beasts, as Sanjaya describes.

questions.q: Apart from Hanuman, what does the epic say accompanied him on the banner?
options: Other fierce, roaring creatures of his retinue
Nothing — the banner bore Hanuman alone
questions.explanation: Sanjaya's descriptions give the standard terrible roaring beings alongside the great monkey.

questions.q: The popular story of Hanuman humbling Arjuna's pride over Rama's bridge — and then joining his banner — belongs to which layer of the tradition?
options: Later and regional retellings, not Vyasa's text
questions.explanation: The Rameshwaram bridge tale is beloved folklore; the Sanskrit epic itself ties the banner to Hanuman's promise to Bhima.

questions.q: In that popular bridge tale, what had Arjuna boasted he could do?
options: Defeat any monkey in single combat
Cross the sea without a bridge
Build a bridge of arrows that could bear an army
questions.explanation: Arjuna's arrow-bridge collapsed under Hanuman's foot until Krishna's grace made it hold, teaching humility.

questions.q: What does Hanuman's presence linking Ramayana and Mahabharata symbolize for tradition?
options: The supremacy of the monkey race
The continuity of divine aid across the ages, as a chiranjivi serves both Rama and Krishna's cause
questions.explanation: Immortal Hanuman bridges the two great epics, serving Vishnu's avatars in both yugas.

questions.q: Even before the great war, in which episode did Arjuna — his ape banner summoned back to the chariot — rout the entire Kaurava host alone?
options: The cattle raid of the Virata war, with Arjuna as Brihannala
questions.explanation: At Virata, Arjuna — emerging from disguise — routed the assembled Kaurava heroes single-handed; his celestial banner blazed anew.

questions.q: When Arjuna reclaimed his weapons at Virata, where had the Pandavas' arms been hidden?
options: In a shami tree, wrapped as a corpse-bundle
questions.explanation: The Pandavas had hung their weapons in a shami tree disguised as a corpse to ward off the curious.

questions.q: What happened to Hanuman at the moment Krishna left the chariot seat?
options: He flew to Kailasa with a cry
He turned to stone upon the flagstaff
He vanished from the banner, his task complete
He entered Arjuna's body as strength
questions.explanation: The epic notes the banner's divine occupant disappeared then — and the chariot, unprotected, burned.

questions.q: Why is the Kapi Dhwaja sometimes invoked in later Hindu practice?
options: As a charm for safe sea voyages
As an emblem of protection and victory where Hanuman's presence guards the devotee
questions.explanation: Flags bearing Hanuman still fly over temples and homes, descendants of the war banner's protective promise.
